There is no information available about the plant Madronella peninsularis. However, I can provide information about Madroña (Arbutus menziesii) or its related species which could be the plant you are referring to. 

Madroña, a shrub or small tree native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America, is a member of the heath family (Ericaceae). The health benefits of Madroña include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, such as skin conditions and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Madroña include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Madroña include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other health issues.
